confirmations.reason: Workspace Rules stored at .agents/rules in the git/workspace root are precisely a repo-scoped instruction file giving the agent standing instructions and context — a direct match.

Plugins are namespaced bundles grouping skills, rules, MCP servers, and hooks into a single package — this directly realizes the capability of bundling reusable capabilities/instructions the agent can load.

Lifecycle Hooks run custom local shell scripts/commands at specific lifecycle points during execution, directly matching the capability of running custom logic on agent events (before/after tool calls, on stop, etc.).

Rules provide persistent, reusable constraints/context but do not specifically target system-prompt shaping, persona definition, or output style customization. Rules are a general instruction mechanism, not a dedicated output/system-prompt customization surface.
Reasoning Model explicitly lets users choose from multiple frontier models to power the agent — a direct realization of model selection capability.
MCP Integration directly implements the Model Context Protocol as the standard way to connect external tools, data, and services — an exact match.
The built-in MCP server manager directly addresses installing, configuring, and managing MCP servers — a direct match for the management & registry capability.
Per-Tool MCP Permission grants agent access to a single named tool on a specific MCP server, directly realizing the capability of controlling/allow-listing which MCP tools the agent may use.

The Subagents feature uses a multi-threaded async architecture to delegate long-running operations to parallel autonomous agents — directly realizing the spawning of focused sub-agents to handle parts of a task.

Multi-Agent Orchestration provides a framework for coordinating multiple agents with error recovery, retries, and coordination logic — directly realizing multi-agent teams that share work and communicate.

Workflows are saved markdown files defining a repeatable series of steps to guide the agent through tasks — a direct and specific match for the capability of defining repeatable, named multi-step task sequences.

Multi-turn plan generation mode explicitly generates multi-turn plans for complex tasks before execution, directly matching the plan-then-execute capability of producing a reviewable plan before the agent edits anything.

Verification loops let the agent run tests and iterate after changes, but this is a reactive correctness loop (run tests → fix errors), not a goal-directed autonomous iteration where a user-defined 'done' condition drives the agent to loop until that verifiable goal is met. The looping is test-result-driven, not goal-condition-driven.
Worktree Support has agents operate in dedicated Git worktrees to isolate parallel work — directly realizing parallel isolated workspaces without collisions.
Background Tasks explicitly run tracked, long-running operations asynchronously without blocking the primary agent — a direct match for background/async execution capability.
Scheduled Tasks allow scheduling messages to agents with repeatable time-based triggers — directly realizing the capability of running the agent on a schedule or trigger without supervision.
Terminal Sandboxing provides kernel-level/native OS isolation for terminal commands executed by the agent — directly realizing sandboxed execution to limit blast radius.
The Fine-Grained Permissions Engine represents every sensitive agent operation as a permission to be approved or denied — a direct realization of tool permission controls.
Independent Network Access Control explicitly controls network connectivity for sandboxed commands independently — directly realizing the capability to restrict the agent's outbound network access.
Inspect Hooks are read-only, non-blocking hooks specifically designed for logging, audit trails, and metrics collection — a direct match for audit logging/telemetry capability.
Quota Tracking lets users monitor their active AI credit quota and consumption directly in the CLI — directly realizing cost and usage tracking capability.

Resuming sessions allows users to return to prior conversations to continue an implementation or refine an existing solution — a direct match for the resume/fork session capability (covers the resume aspect; fork is not mentioned but the core capability is realized).
